- '[[openalex-2605.21295-timesrl-generalizable-time-series-behavioral-modeling-via-se]]'
title: Faithfulness of semantic summaries
---

**Background:** Semantic summaries generated by LLMs from raw sensor data often involve lossy compression, potentially prioritizing decision-relevant patterns over clinical faithfulness or longitudinal completeness.

**Question / Future Work:** A rigorous investigation is required to disentangle whether these generated abstractions are faithful representations of the underlying behavioral dynamics or if they merely collapse data into patterns optimized for specific downstream prediction tasks. Evaluating the trade-off between task-specific utility and long-term diagnostic faithfulness is essential for trust in clinical deployment.

**Why It Matters:** Determining whether latent behavioral representations are faithful to patient history is critical for ensuring the safety and interpretability of LLM-driven diagnostic systems.
